narration: Define an array named one23 containing the values 1, 2, 3.
Use the for...each method to iterate through the array, logging each item and its index to the console.
Create a new array named doubled by mapping the original array, where each item is multiplied by 2.
Explain that filter checks each item against a condition, returning a new array with items that satisfy the condition.
Use filter to create a new array containing only even numbers from the input array by checking if item % 2 === 0.
Explain that reduce takes an array, accumulates a result, and passes the result and the next item to the next iteration.
Explain that sum checks if any item in the array meets a specific condition, returning true or false.
Use sum to check if any item in the array is less than zero.
Explain that every checks if all items in the array meet a specific condition, returning true or false.
Use every to check if all items in the array are greater than zero.
Explain that find iterates through the array and returns the first item that matches a condition, or undefined if no match is found.
Use find to search for an object with an ID equal to 'B' in an array of objects.
Explain that findIndex is similar to find but returns the index of the matching item instead of the item itself.
Use findIndex to locate the index of the object with ID 'B' in the array.
